title: Could Black Women Win Best Supporting Actress Three Years in a Row?
text: The 2026 Oscar race presents a historic possibility: if either Teyana Taylor or Wunmi Mosaku wins Best Supporting Actress, it would mark the first time in the Academy's 98-year history that Black women have won best supporting actress three years in a row. This potential achievement would follow victories by Da'Vine Joy Randolph (The Holdovers) and Afro-Latina Zoe Saldaña (Emilia Pérez) in previous years. This milestone has never occurred in any major entertainment awards body. Teyana Taylor (One Battle After Another) has secured 10 precursor awards, including the Golden Globe. Wunmi Mosaku (Sinners) trails by one award. The article emphasizes the stark underrepresentation of Black women in Oscar history: only 20 of 3,100+ statuettes have gone to Black women—roughly 0.6% of all awards. A three-consecutive-year sweep would signal sustained industry recognition rather than isolated moments.
